propositions: Retired but still-set env vars and TOML keys affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, security, or lifecycle policy are silently defaulted or ignored, giving no runtime signal of the mismatch.

A RemovedInput tombstone registry with separate kind/behavior vocabularies turns retired inputs into targeted migration errors (old name, replacement, removed-in version, nonzero exit) while unknown keys keep the forward-compat policy.
The error-class tombstone uses RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so it is not swallowed by tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError or MCP boot's broad except Exception handlers.
Env-var and file-presence tombstone checks must fire at the top of load_config() before the cache lookup so they trigger on warm MCP cache hits.
TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR is checked inside tracker_dir_override() so all resolution paths bypassing load_config() also fail closed.
A config validate / doctor command uses scan_tombstones() to report all matches without raising, and a CI golden-file guard fails if a key is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry.
